title: Chapter 42: The Redemption
content: Roxanne stood in the heart of the Primordial Void, holding the black card.
"How do I redeem it?"
*You already know. You've known since the beginning. The card is not money. It is a promise. A promise that the Void would return. To redeem the card is to call the Void back into existence."
"And if I do that?"
*The Nether-Grid will recognize the original contract as active again. The Exchange's authority will be invalidated. The 11th Layer's enforcement will be void. The First Seat's power will end."
"What's the catch?"
*A pause.

*The redemption will consume the card. It will become part of the Void again. You will no longer have infinite wealth. You will have a restored Primordial system. A system without debt. Without enforcement. Without chains."
Roxanne looked at the card. It had been her companion for six months. Her weapon. Her shield. Her identity.
"I'll lose the card."
*You will lose the card. But you will gain what the card always represented."
She was silent for a long moment.

Then she thought of Lena, sketching in the quad. Of the Executioner, standing guard at the gate. Of the refugees, building homes in the dormitories. Of Silas, who had been waiting four billion years for someone to buy him free.
"Do it."
*Are you certain?*
"No. But I'm doing it anyway."
The Void around her shifted. Contracted. The card began to glow — not its usual steady pulse, but a bright, building light.
And then the card dissolved.
Not into pieces. Into light. Into the fabric of the Void itself.
The light spread outward, filling the emptiness, threading through the nothing like veins of gold through dark stone.
Roxanne watched her infinite wealth become a world.
*The Primordial Void is restored,* the voice said. *The original contract is active. The Nether-Grid will feel it."
Roxanne looked at her empty hand.
"I hope this works."
She turned and walked back through the door.
